question_title: What will be the value of "Secured Overnight Financing Rate" on 2026-03-25?
summary: SOFR on March 25, 2026 is almost entirely determined by the Fed Funds target rate. The FOMC meets March 17-18, 2026, with CME FedWatch showing 96% probability of holding rates at 3.50-3.75%. If rates are held (overwhelmingly likely), SOFR should remain near its current stable level of 3.64-3.65%. The main uncertainty comes from: (1) a 4% chance of a 25bp rate cut that would drop SOFR to ~3.40, (2) quarter-end proximity effects (March 25 is 4 business days before quarter-end) that could add 0.02-0.08% upward pressure, and (3) repo market dynamics amid geopolitical stress (US-Israel-Iran conflict, oil at ~$100). The distribution is heavily concentrated around 3.65-3.66% with a small left tail for the cut scenario.

factors.description: CME FedWatch shows 96% probability Fed holds rates at 3.50-3.75% at March 18 FOMC meeting. SOFR closely tracks Fed Funds rate, meaning no change keeps SOFR near current ~3.65%.
SOFR has been extremely stable in current regime since Dec 2025 rate cut: mean 3.648, std 0.007 over last 6 observations (3.64-3.65). Very low daily volatility when policy is unchanged.

Quarter-end proximity effect: March 25 is 4 business days before March 31 quarter-end. Historical data shows small premium of 0.03-0.09% at this distance from quarter-end. Slight upward pressure possible.
Small (4%) chance of 25bp rate cut at March 18 FOMC. If cut occurs, SOFR would drop to ~3.39-3.40%. Creates a discrete left tail in the distribution.
Geopolitical stress (US-Israel attacks on Iran, oil near $100/barrel) could cause repo market stress pushing SOFR slightly higher, but Fed standing repo facility limits upside.

reasoning:
**Forecast: SOFR on March 25, 2026**

The distribution is tightly concentrated around 3.65-3.66% because SOFR is policy-determined:

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 3.63% |
| P20 | 3.64% |
| P40 | 3.65% |
| P60 | 3.66% |
| P80 | 3.68% |
| P90 | 3.69% |

**Key reasoning:**
- **Fed hold (96% prob):** The FOMC meets March 17-18 with overwhelming market expectation of no change. Current target: 3.50-3.75%. SOFR has been stable at 3.64-3.65% since early March.
- **Quarter-end proximity:** March 25 is 4 business days before quarter-end, which historically adds a small premium (0.03-0.09%) — this slightly tilts the distribution upward.
- **Cut tail risk (4%):** A surprise 25bp cut would drop SOFR to ~3.40%, creating the left tail. This is why P10 extends to 3.63 rather than concentrating even tighter.
- **Geopolitical context:** Iran conflict and oil at $100 create inflation pressure that reinforces the "hold" expectation, making a cut even less likely.
sources_consulted: [FOMC Meeting In March 2026: Fed Rate Decision, Dot Plot, And What It Means For Bitcoin | MEXC](https://blog.mexc.com/news/fomc-meeting-in-march-2026-fed-rate-decision-dot-plot-and-what-it-means-for-bitcoin/)
[(Metaculus) What will be the value of "Secured Overnight Financing Rate" on 2026-03-25?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42671)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series SOFR once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=SOFR`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-03-25 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
